_meta.name: ALM 14B r8a fast-pod direct MFU measurement
_meta.task: Direct measurement of r8a stacked config on fast H100 pod — prior r8a measurements were on slow pod (48.1%) with fast-pod extrapolated at 68-69%. This run replaces the extrapolation with ground truth.

stack.note: Exact version pin match to holo pod (216.243.220.217:17992) where r8a was originally developed. liger_kernel from pypi (not cu128 index).

env.note: ALM_COMPILE_UNBLOCK=1 set but gated off under --unsloth per train_alm_14b_r8a.py L363; documents stack intent only. compile_unblock is effectively NOT active in this run.

gpu_telemetry.source: nvidia-smi dmon -s pucvmet -c 120 -d 5 (30 active samples during training)
gpu_telemetry.note: Compute-bound: SM 95.5% and power 93% of TDP simultaneously — the GPU is genuinely saturated. No headroom for software speedup on this pod.

mfu_computation.formula_basis: Kaplan/Chinchilla 6N FLOPs per training token (fwd+bwd). Matches r7 published MFU derivation in shared/state/alm14b_r8_ceiling_confirm_20260415.json.

final_cumulative_step150.comment: Cumulative from step 1 — includes initial Unsloth JIT warmup (step 1 @ 13.0 step/min)

steady_best_estimate.comment: Asymptotic steady-state (curve saturates at 41.5, estimated 42.0 for per-step marginal cost excluding residual warmup)

mfu_computation.headline_basis: window_step120_150 — most accurate steady-state without warmup contamination

r8a_slow_pod_stack.pod: holo 216.243.220.217 (sweep measurement of same stack as this run)

verdict.r8a_stack_improvement_over_r7: NEGATIVE — 51.4% vs r7 62.6%, regression of 11.2 pp
verdict.r8a_stack_improvement_over_slow_pod_r8a: +3.3 pp (48.1% → 51.4%) — fast pod is modestly faster than holo for same stack
verdict.fast_pod_estimate_validity: REJECTED — prior 'fast pod ~68-69%' extrapolation from slow-pod 48.1% was too optimistic. Real fast-pod r8a ceiling is 51.4%, well under r7's 62.6% on an older (now-terminated) pod.
verdict.pod_silicon_variance_evidence: r7 pod (ubzp169igb7tns) hit 62.6% on unsloth-only (simpler stack). This fast pod (jmejpw0x7j6wbx) hits 51.4% on r8a stacked (unsloth+liger+lora_dropout=0). Same H100 SXM 80GB model, different silicon/firmware/cooling → ~11 pp MFU delta. RunPod H100 fleet is HETEROGENEOUS.
verdict.alm_bf16_ceiling_conclusion: Ceiling depends on pod. For this particular fast pod, the achievable bf16 MFU under stacked r8a is 51.4% — not 68-69% as extrapolated. Gap to the 80% practical ceiling is 28.6 pp on this silicon. r7's 62.6% on a lost pod remains the highest measured, but not reproducible on demand.
verdict.compute_bound_verification: YES — SM 95.5% and power 93.4% of TDP. The GPU is running flat out. The 51.4% MFU is the physical ceiling of THIS silicon under THIS stack, not a software leak.

artifacts.pod_state_after_run: RETAINED per instructions — pod not deleted, user makes stop/delete call
